info.parenttitle: A3.3 Introduction to mechanical systems (HL only)

markdown:
<definition term="Mechanical System">A set of **physical components** that **convert input force and motion** into a **desired output**.</definition>

## Input–Process–Output Systems


Mechanical systems are made up of three key elements:

1. **Input**: The energy, force, or data introduced into a system.
2. **Process**: The transformation or manipulation of the input to achieve a desired outcome.
3. **Output**: The final result or product of the system's operation.

<callout type="note">This model is not limited to **mechanical** systems, it applies to **digital**, **biological**, and **organisational** systems as well.</callout>

### Inputs


1. **Energy Sources**:
  1. **Electrical**: Powers motors and actuators.
  2. **Mechanical**: Includes human force or gravitational energy.
  3. **Hydraulic/Pneumatic**: Uses fluid or air pressure.
2. **Sensors and Data**:
  1. **Temperature Sensors**: Trigger cooling systems.
  2. **Pressure Sensors**: Regulate hydraulic systems.

<callout type="example">In a **car engine**, the **input** includes **fuel** (chemical energy) and **air** (oxygen) mixed in precise ratios.</callout>

![Pressing the switch to turn on the air conditioner acts as the input (Boonstra)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/c71dfd26c4c70f573ae312ca38f1fdcd6c75ec3a-1254x836.png)

### Processes


1. **Transformation of Energy**:
  1. **Combustion**: Converts chemical energy to mechanical energy in engines.
  2. **Electromechanical Conversion**: Motors transform electrical energy into motion.
2. **Control and Regulation**:
  1. **Feedback Loops**: Sensors adjust processes in real-time.
  2. **Valves and Switches**: Regulate flow and pressure.
3. **Mechanical Operations**:
  1. **Gearing**: Alters speed and torque.
  2. **Linkages**: Convert linear motion to rotational motion.

<callout type="tip">When analyzing a **mechanical process**, identify the **key components** and their **roles** in transforming inputs to outputs.</callout>

![The gear mechanism (process) transforms the input motion from the engine into a controlled output to the wheels (howstuffworks)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/7d877587f61a49e700bbc3264e68dd84061996a2-290x259.png)

### Outputs


1. **Mechanical Motion**:
  1. **Rotational**: Wheels, gears, turbines.
  2. **Linear**: Pistons, conveyor belts.
2. **Work Performed**:
  1. **Lifting**: Cranes and elevators.
  2. **Cutting**: CNC machines and saws.
3. **Feedback Data**:
  1. **Work Performed**:
      1. **Lifting**: Cranes and elevators.
    2. **Cutting**: CNC machines and saws.
  2. **Feedback Data**:
      1. **Performance Metrics**: Efficiency, speed, temperature.

![In a wind turbine, the output is electrical energy generated from the rotational motion of the blades. (energia)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/1961511852d8a91ce11bdd2ab45f8cd63dbc5e7e-318x159.png)

### Real-World Application


<callout type="example">### Hand-Operated Drill


* **Input:** The user turns the handle.
* **Process:** The gear mechanism multiplies the motion.
* **Output:** The drill bit rotates and cuts into the material.

![Hand-operated drill ](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/c4765c65599e1b9e4d9d9af5b18f6ecbc2ba272c-612x612.png)</callout>

<callout type="self_review">Identify the input, process, and output in a bicycle's braking system. How does each component contribute to the overall function?

![Parts of the drivetrain](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/a8f8893d38c734b4422d4970a42f92b0e3bf3a6d-1300x817.png)</callout>
